finding: Native emergency commits can easily preserve ATM-Actor trailers while still using a stale Git author/committer from the host repo. ATM diagnostics also surfaced safeModeActor/ambient identity values that did not match the active captain actor.
expectedBehavior: Emergency commit guidance should set and verify Git author/committer identity atomically from the actor identity, and ATM post-commit/pre-push diagnostics should elevate mismatches into an actionable recoveryCommand rather than advisory-only drift.
evidenceOrRepro: Codex emergency commit for ATM-GOV-0260 initially landed with Author/Committer `codex-gpt-5.4-mini` while trailers said `ATM-Actor: codex-plan31-captain`; it required an amend to repair actor continuity. Pre-push later reported safeModeActor `codex-gpt-5.4-mini`.
followUp: Add identity continuity checks for emergency/native fallback paths and a generated copyable command that sets GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL/GIT_COMMITTER_* before commit.
